description:
Get C# compiler and analyzer diagnostics (errors/warnings) from the Roslyn workspace — the same diagnostics an IDE shows. Use this to verify your edits to .cs files compile before applying them in Unity; checking is read-only and does not recompile or reload scripts in the Editor.

Scopes: 'file' (default when file_path is given) is fast; 'workspace' covers the whole solution and catches cross-file breakage (e.g. a renamed method breaking callers), but the first workspace query after project load can take minutes on large solutions.

When Unity analyzers are enabled in settings, results include Unity-specific rules (UNT*): MonoBehaviour misuse, null-coalescing on UnityEngine.Object, incorrect event function signatures, expensive calls, and more.

File scope additionally validates the file's project string references — tags (CompareTag/.tag), layers (NameToLayer/GetMask), scene loads by name/path/index (against build settings), Resources.Load* paths, and legacy Input axes (flagged as guaranteed runtime exceptions when the project enables the new Input System only). These compile fine and only fail at runtime; problems are appended as a 'Project string refs' section with did-you-mean suggestions. Workspace scope does NOT run this string-ref validation — it covers compiler/analyzer diagnostics only.

file_path.description: Workspace-relative or absolute path to a .cs file. Omit to query the whole workspace.

scope.description: 'file' or 'workspace'. Default: 'file' when file_path is given, otherwise 'workspace'.

min_severity.description: Minimum severity to report, that severity and above ('warning' = errors + warnings): 'error', 'warning' (default), 'info' or 'hint'.

max_results.description: Maximum number of diagnostics to return. Default 100, max 400.
